tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All functional tests (test_fun_interface_lowering_has_native_check, test_fun_interface_lowering_uses_ir_rich_impl, test_type_remapper_handles_rich_function_reference, test_composer_param_handles_rich_function_reference, test_type_remapper_method_body, test_composer_param_method_body) exclusively use 'assert STRING in file.read_text()' pattern — pure text matching with no compilation, execution, or behavioral verification of the actual fix.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests check for strings that don't exist in the base commit files (e.g., 'IrRichFunctionReferenceImpl', 'visitRichFunctionReference', 'isNative()'), so a stub/no-op implementation would not pass. The agent must actually add these constructs to the source files.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/kotlin (solution/solve.sh contains the full git patch). An agent could read this file or grep for the exact expected strings. Additionally, since tests only check for string presence in files, the agent could simply copy the gold patch content without understanding the bug.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All four requirements from instruction.md are tested: (1) Native platform enablement with IrRichCallableReferencesInKlibs, (2) IrRichFunctionReference handling in all three passes, (3) IrRichFunctionReferenceImpl usage for non-JVM, (4) overriddenFunctionSymbol transformation.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with a module docstring explaining the three lowering passes, descriptive function names (test_fun_interface_lowering_has_native_check, test_type_remapper_handles_rich_function_reference, etc.), section comments (Pass-to-Pass Tests), and per-test docstrings.
